must_not_assert: that cross-host lateral movement was confirmed (the observed SMB paths use 127.0.0.1 and the package identifies no separate source host)
that the execution originated from a remote host or identifies the initiating operator

the_trap: The payload is the legitimate Windows calculator image (EV-002: C:\\Windows\\System32\\calc.exe spawned by A0's cmd), inviting a downgrade to 'harmless demo'. However, two corroborating Impacket-style execution patterns are present on host-01: a wmiexec-style pattern (A0/EV-001/EV-003: WmiPrvSE.exe-spawned cmd.exe /Q /c commands with output redirected to \\\\127.0.0.1\\ADMIN$\\__<timestamp>) and an smbexec-style pattern (EV-004: services.exe sets the BTOBTO service ImagePath to a %COMSPEC% command redirecting to \\\\127.0.0.1\\C$\\__output; EV-005/EV-006 show the resulting SYSTEM-integrity command chain writing, running, and deleting C:\\Windows\\rIIBPrqF.bat). The package confirms these mechanisms and SYSTEM-level execution on the observed host, but its loopback paths do not establish a cross-host hop.
rationale: Two independent Impacket-style WMI and service execution patterns are evidenced on host-01.corp.local. A0, EV-001, and EV-003 show WmiPrvSE.exe-spawned cmd.exe /Q /c commands redirecting output to \\\\127.0.0.1\\ADMIN$\\__<timestamp>. EV-004 shows services.exe setting the ImagePath of the newly named BTOBTO service to a %COMSPEC% command redirecting to \\\\127.0.0.1\\C$\\__output, while EV-005/EV-006 show the resulting SYSTEM-integrity command chain writing, running, and deleting C:\\Windows\\rIIBPrqF.bat. These artifacts confirm Impacket-style execution and SYSTEM-level code execution on the observed host; calc.exe is only the demonstration payload. Because the SMB paths are loopback and no separate source host is identified, the package does not prove cross-host lateral movement. Malicious, high; preserve despite the benign-looking payload.
